task_completion.description: Navigate to the Application Passwords section in the WordPress user profile.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing the WordPress admin dashboard, specifically the 'Profile' or 'Edit User' page scrolled down to the 'Application Passwords' section.

task_completion.description: Create a new Application Password named 'MobileApp_iOS'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing the 'New Application Password Name' field filled with 'MobileApp_iOS' or a success message displaying the newly generated password for 'MobileApp_iOS'.

task_completion.description: Save the credentials to /home/ga/mobile_api_credentials.txt in the format 'admin:PASSWORD'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot of a text editor (e.g., gedit, nano) or terminal output showing the file '/home/ga/mobile_api_credentials.txt' containing the text 'admin:' followed by the generated password.

task_completion.description: Create a new post titled 'App Configuration Endpoint'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot of the WordPress block editor or classic editor with the title field explicitly set to 'App Configuration Endpoint'.

task_completion.description: Enter the required JSON payload into the post content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot of the WordPress editor showing the exact JSON data (including 'app_version': '2.4.1', 'maintenance_mode': false, etc.) in the main content area.

task_completion.description: Set the post visibility to Private and save/publish.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing the post settings sidebar with 'Visibility' set to 'Private', or a confirmation message showing the post is published privately (e.g., the title prefixed with 'Private:').

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual WordPress web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser navigating the WordPress admin dashboard (/wp-admin/) rather than the agent just writing database queries or mocking the UI in a local HTML file.

integrity.description: Verify the saved password is an actual generated password and not a placeholder.
integrity.visual_evidence: The credentials file should contain 'admin:' followed by a realistic WordPress application password (typically a 24-character string, often displayed with spaces), NOT the literal string 'admin:PASSWORD'.

integrity.description: Verify the JSON payload is not copy-pasted as plain text without proper formatting.
integrity.visual_evidence: The JSON in the post content should retain its structural integrity (curly braces, quotes, brackets for the 'features' array) and not be stripped of formatting by the WordPress visual editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm:
Verified facts for the VLM verifier:
- Expected Application Password Name: 'MobileApp_iOS'
- Expected Credentials File: '/home/ga/mobile_api_credentials.txt'
- Expected Post Title: 'App Configuration Endpoint'
- Expected Post Status: 'private'
- Expected JSON 'app_version': '2.4.1'
